_comment: Configuration for the ZonePRO web control panel. Edit this file, then restart the server (npm start).

zonepro._comment: IP address and port of the dbx ZonePRO unit. Port 3804 is fixed by dbx.

protocol._comment_srcDevice: Virtual device address the controller identifies itself as. 0x0033 is the value dbx documents for a 3rd party controller.
protocol._comment_destDevice: Virtual device address of the ZonePRO unit itself (from your captured strings). Same for every zone on the same physical unit.
protocol._comment_svIds: Router object state variables: 0x0000 = Input Source, 0x0001 = Master Fader (volume), 0x0002 = Master Mute. These are fixed by dbx for router objects and are the same for every zone.
protocol._comment_range: Fader range is -90dB (value 1) to +20dB (value 221) in 0.5dB steps. The slider UI works entirely in dB; the server converts to the raw ZonePRO value.

_comment_inputs: Default list of selectable sources shown in every zone's input dropdown, value = the raw byte the ZonePRO expects for that source on a router object (0 = no source selected). This list is a starting point from the dbx guide's own example router -- the actual sources and their numbers depend entirely on your ZonePRO Designer configuration, so rename/reorder/add/remove entries here to match your setup (capture a MultiSVSet for each source in the Network Trace window to find its number, same way Zone 1's object ID was found). Set this to an empty array [] (or a zone's own "inputs": []) to hide the SRC dropdown everywhere, or for just that zone.
_comment_zones: Object ID is the ULONG router object address for that zone's output, e.g. '0x01050018'. Get it from ZonePRO Designer: click the router object for that zone and press Ctrl+Shift+O (see the 1-way RS-232 guide, Section 3). Zones with object=null are left out of the page entirely until you fill them in. A zone can optionally have its own "inputs": [...] list (same shape as the top-level one) if that zone's sources differ from the default list above.
